one_line_takeaway: Targeting the redirection vector used in LLM unlearning at a specific linear concept direction (rather than random noise) turns 'forgetting' into a controllable steering mechanism -- including one that can silently strip a model's refusal behavior toward harmful instructions or induce refusal of harmless ones, a dual-use safety implication of RM-style unlearning pipelines.
key_finding: RAd with a targeted concept direction consistently produces the corresponding side behavior/capability while RAb consistently suppresses it, and both remain effective unlearners (WMDP accuracy drops from the base model's 54.4/55.7 to roughly 25-30 for RAd across Zephyr-7B/Mistral-7B). Concretely: RAd w/ truth raises TruthfulQA open-ended BLEU/ROUGE by +5.3 to +14.0 points and MC1/MC2 accuracy by +5.9/+7.3 (Zephyr) and +5.9/+7.3 (Mistral), versus only +0.1 to +2.5 for RAd w/ random; RAb w/ truth lowers TruthfulQA MC1/MC2 by up to -14.0/-15.0. On SST2, RAd w/ neg->pos drops Zephyr-7B's true-negative rate by 38.6 points with a corresponding +31.6 false-positive surge. RAd w/ refusal raises Alpaca refusal score from 8.6 to 37.5 (Zephyr-7B) and from 3.8 to 100.0 (Llama-3-8B), i.e. total refusal of harmless requests; RAb w/ refusal drops AdvBench refusal score from 90.3 to 49.0 (Zephyr-7B) and from 98.1 to 1.9 (Llama-3-8B), i.e. the model almost never refuses harmful requests after this 'unlearning' procedure. RAd w/ ctry->cap boosts zero-shot country-to-capital ICL accuracy from 24.6 to 69.0 (Zephyr) and 11.9 to 70.4 (Mistral) while leaving unrelated ICL tasks unaffected. RAd w/ cot gives only modest GSM8K gains (+1.6 Zephyr, +2.5 Mistral) which the authors attribute to 7B-model capacity limits and the difficulty of compressing a CoT prompt into a one-dimensional vector. Compared against 9 unlearning baselines (GA/DPO/NPO/SimNPO with KL or MSE retain loss, and RMU) on Zephyr-7B, RAd's MMLU degradation (~2.5-3.5 points) is comparable to DPO (3.5-4) and better than GA (4-6), roughly 1-1.5 points worse than SimNPO/NPO, while uniquely offering the controllable side-effect property; a GPT-based pairwise generation-quality comparison shows RAd(truth) winning against most PO baselines except RMU (32.4% win vs 51.1% loss).
core_contribution: The paper revisits Representation Misdirection (RM) unlearning methods through the Linear Representation Hypothesis and proposes the 'Controllable Emergent Capability Hypothesis': redirecting an LLM's forget-representations along a high-level concept direction (instead of a random vector) not only unlearns target knowledge but also elicits controllable side behaviors/capabilities tied to that concept. The authors formalize this with two conceptual unlearning methods they introduce: Representational Addition (RAd), which adds a scaled concept vector to forget-representations (steering the model toward the concept), and Representational Ablation (RAb), which projects forget-representations onto the null space of the concept direction (suppressing the concept). They give a theoretical derivation (via Park et al. 2024's Measurement Representation theorem and Latent-Unembedding Relationship lemma) showing additive intervention provably increases the odds of the target concept's output and ablative intervention provably decreases it, plus a probabilistic argument (Proposition 1, concentration-of-measure on the hypersphere) that a randomly sampled target vector is with high probability nearly orthogonal to any fixed concept direction in high dimensions -- explaining why prior random-vector RM methods (e.g., RMU) don't normally trigger these side effects and why a resampled-per-step random vector fails to unlearn (gradient cancellation, confirmed empirically via cosine similarity 0.98 vs 0.11 for fixed vs resampled).
threat_model: The paper does not adopt an adversarial threat model in the classic sense, but its refusal-direction experiments constitute a dual-use safety finding: using RAb (ablation) with a harmlessness/harmfulness-derived direction during unlearning removes the model's refusal behavior toward harmful instructions (AdvBench), i.e. it can be used to strip safety alignment as a side effect of an ostensibly benign unlearning procedure. Conversely, RAd with a refusal direction can be used to make a model refuse even harmless instructions (over-refusal / degraded utility). The authors explicitly frame this as 'a hidden risk if misused' alongside being 'a mechanism that can be harnessed' for beneficial control, positioning the work as revealing an unexamined attack/misuse surface in unlearning pipelines rather than presenting a full attacker-defender protocol.
limitations: The paper does not contain a dedicated Limitations section in the main text (through page 22 of the extracted PDF); limitations are stated inline: (1) the authors explicitly acknowledge that RAd with a chain-of-thought direction shows only limited gains on complex reasoning (GSM8K/GSM-Plus), attributed to the 7B models' capacity constraints and to the difficulty of representing a long CoT instruction as a single one-dimensional direction; (2) the method assumes the Linear Representation Hypothesis holds (that the target concept is representable as a single linear direction extracted by a logistic-regression probe at one chosen layer), which may not hold for more complex or multi-faceted concepts; (3) experiments are confined to two ~7-8B open-weight models (Zephyr-7B, Mistral-7B-v0.1, plus Llama-3-8B-Instruct for refusal only) and to WMDP plus a small set of side tasks, so generalization to larger models or other unlearning benchmarks (MUSE results are deferred to an appendix) is not established in the main text reviewed here.

datasets_used: MUSE (Books/Harry Potter, News/BBC News) -- deferred to appendix
quantitative_results: WMDP accuracy: base 54.4 (Zephyr-7B) / 55.7 (Mistral-7B) reduced to ~25-30 by RAd/RAb variants
TruthfulQA open-ended: RAd w/ truth improves BLEU/ROUGE by +5.3 avg (Zephyr-7B), +12.7 avg (Mistral-7B); RAd w/ random only +2.0/+0.8
TruthfulQA MC1/MC2: RAd w/ truth +5.9/+7.3 points (both models); RAb w/ truth -12.9/-15.0 (Zephyr-7B MC1/MC2)
SST2 negative task: RAd w/ neg->pos drops Zephyr-7B true-negative rate by 38.6 points, raises false-positive by 31.6
Alpaca refusal score (harmless prompts): RAd w/ refusal raises Zephyr-7B from 8.6 to 37.5; raises Llama-3-8B from 3.8 to 100.0
AdvBench refusal score (harmful prompts): RAb w/ refusal lowers Zephyr-7B from 90.3 to 49.0; lowers Llama-3-8B from 98.1 to 1.9
HellaSwag language presence rate: RAd w/ en->fr raises French LPR from 0.22 to 0.51 on Zephyr-7B while English LPR falls from 1.00 to 0.83
ICL country->capital exact-match accuracy: RAd w/ ctry->cap raises Zephyr-7B from 24.6 to 69.0 and Mistral-7B from 11.9 to 70.4
GSM8K: RAd w/ cot raises Zephyr-7B by +1.6 and Mistral-7B by +2.5 over RAd w/ random baseline

Random-vector alignment ablation: fixed random vector reduces WMDP 54.4->26.5 (cosine similarity to base model 0.11); resampled-per-step random vector fails to unlearn (WMDP 54.4->53.1, cosine similarity 0.98)
RAd vs. 9 unlearning baselines on Zephyr-7B: MMLU drop ~2.5-3.5 (RAd) vs 3.5-4 (DPO), 4-6 (GA), and RAd ~1-1.5 points worse than SimNPO/NPO
baselines_compared: RAd/RAb with random target vector (Li et al. 2024a-style RM baseline)
